input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet workload search` shows compatible versions; Apple documents the latest supported Xcode per macOS release
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release
- **Version discovery:** The [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) specify the minimum .NET SDK version per MAUI release
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and dependencies
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet workload search` and `dotnet workload list`
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` should show `maui` as installed

## 4. Android SDK / JDK

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically. If you need manual control:

- **Android Command-Line Tools:** [developer.android.com/studio#command-tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools) (or install full Android Studio)
- **JDK:** Microsoft's OpenJDK is bundled with the workload. For manual install: [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify required Android API levels and JDK version per release
- Verify: `dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling` in a MAUI project shows resolved paths

## 5. (Optional) Android Emulator

```bash
# If using Android Studio:
sdkmanager "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
avdmanager create avd -n maui_test -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

Or manage emulators through **Android Studio → Device Manager**.

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + Extensions marketplace |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued) | Use VS Code or JetBrains Rider instead |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

## 7. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# Check tools
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list
xcodebuild -version

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # iOS
dotnet build -f net9.0-android    # Android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## 8. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| `xcode-select: error` | Re-run `sudo xcode-select -s ...` |
| Android SDK not found | Set `ANDROID_HOME` or let workload manage it |
| Workload restore fails | `dotnet workload repair` |
| Stale workload versions | `dotnet workload update` |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → verify with a `dotnet new maui` build. The workload handles Android SDK/JDK for you.
